name: The Thologolong Foundation Cow
bloodline_detail: The original roan Shorthorn house cow at Thologolong station, upper Murray River.
notable: The founding dam of the entire breed: bred to Aberdeen Angus bulls at Thologolong (c.1905), she produced the run of silver-grey calves that bred true and became the Murray Grey. One source records her name as 'Strawberry' — single-sourced, so treated as tradition rather than settled fact.
notes: Origin story well-corroborated; the name 'Strawberry' appears in only one association source.

name: The Thologolong Angus Foundation Bull
bloodline_detail: Purebred Aberdeen Angus bull(s) run by Peter Sutherland at Thologolong from the 1890s.
notable: The Angus sire behind the first grey calves. No individual name survives in the record — Sutherland ran a succession of Angus bulls over his Shorthorn cows. Aberdeen Angus is one of the two foundation breeds of the Murray Grey.
notes: No individual bull name is recorded in any source.

name: The Gadd Grey Bull
bloodline_detail: Grey bull from Thologolong, taken on by Mervyn Gadd at 'The Glen', Mt Alfred.
notable: Acquired c.1939 'for the price of a steer' and run over 30 commercial Angus females — the first hard commercial proof of the breed. His grey-cross progeny topping the Newmarket saleyards in the late 1940s is what earned the cattle the name 'Murray Grey'.
notes: Never officially named; well-documented in the breed's histories.

name: Helen Sutherland's Michaelong Herd
bloodline_detail: The eight foundation females Helen Sutherland bought at the 1932 Thologolong dispersal.
notable: The systematic breeding program that turned a colour curiosity into a breed. Helen Player Sutherland selected for fertility, mothering, growth, carcase and temperament; rebuilt after the 1945 drought cut the herd to eight; made a Life Member of the Society in 1971. She is credited across three countries as the breed's founder.

name: Willalooka Power
bloodline_detail: Willalooka Stud, Keith, South Australia — a leading Murray Grey stud for decades.
notable: A modern multi-trait leader (weight, scrotal size, carcase weight) whose genetics run through pedigrees on both sides of the Pacific — a foundational influence on the contemporary breed via AI.
notes: From the American Murray Grey Association 2025 Sire Directory and Willalooka records.

name: Monterey T Bone T55
bloodline_detail: Monterey Stud, Karridale, Western Australia; sire Southend Narssist.
notable: Top price of A$25,000 at the 32nd Monterey Murray Grey & Angus sale, March 2024 — the best-documented modern Murray Grey sale record. Sold as a ¾ share plus a half-share in international semen rights to the Maefair (NSW) and Lindsay (Vic) studs.
notes: Named bull, named sire, dated primary news source (Farm Weekly).

name: Wallawong Xyris LEJ J16
bloodline_detail: Wallawong Stud, NSW; sire JOS A32.
notable: Top-priced bull at Wallawong's 2015 on-property sale at A$20,000; later appears as a grandsire in North American pedigrees. Wallawong was the historic first stud to export Murray Grey semen to the United States.

name: Cadfor Falcon N2
bloodline_detail: Cadfor Stud, Binda, NSW; ET son of the Cadfor Beroleanne D138 female line.
notable: Top 1% of the breed for 200-, 400- and 600-day weight and multiple carcase traits — a modern trait-leading sire from one of the breed's most respected contemporary studs.
